topic: Harden and refine the mk-spec-memory daemon/startup/MCP issues surfaced in the 031 packet: (1) audit for other unaudited call sites of the persistQualityLoopContent scan-write-back bug class beyond startupScan/file-watcher/force-reindex; (2) root-cause and fix the OpenCode MCP 'server unavailable/failed' transient startup race for mk-spec-memory; (3) fix the model-server-supervision.cjs sun_path-overflow latent bug (falls back to an over-104-byte macOS socket path when HF_EMBED_SERVER_URL/SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR are absent); (4) diagnose and harden the single-writer lock contention on context-index.sqlite that caused a memory_index_scan to hang 30 minutes with no response; (5) general daemon startup/lease/re-election robustness given heavy concurrent-session usage of this shared repo.

diff_summary: Appended bounded pre-init research context for the daemon/startup/MCP hardening topic.

focus: Audit indexMemoryFile/indexSingleFile callers for residual persistQualityLoopContent source write-back
noveltyJustification: Closed the production caller inventory, confirmed async ingest as one residual write-back path, and ruled out embedding retry and checkpoint-derived rebuild paths.

graphEvents.label: Residual source write-back callers
Async ingest defaults to direct origin
Crash recovery replays ingest paths
Retry and checkpoint paths do not source-index
context-server ingest callback
ingest job crash recovery

focus: Trace mk-spec-memory launcher startup and warm-owner bridge timing against OpenCode's observed failure window
noveltyJustification: Converted the timeout-race hypothesis into a branch-specific timing model, identified two serial deep probes plus an unbounded macOS ps call, and ruled out bootstrap and model-listener work on the warm-owner path.

graphEvents.label: Launcher and OpenCode startup timeout mechanism
Warm-owner path performs two serial deep probes
macOS owner classification uses unbounded synchronous ps
Bootstrap and demand listener excluded from warm-owner handshake
Launcher and IPC bridge startup sequence
Session proxy readiness loop

focus: Determine real invocation reachability and the safest permanent fallback for the model-server sun_path overflow
noveltyJustification: Identified a real plugin bridge that can omit both socket variables, distinguished that launcher reachability from normally masked model-bind reachability, closed the resolver inventory, and independently confirmed the source write-back caller inventory.

graphEvents.label: Model-server socket fallback reachability and fix
Normal MCP and CLI paths use short targets
Advisor plugin bridge can omit both socket variables
Canonical shared short fallback should be independent of dbDir
Iteration 1 write-back inventory independently confirmed
Model-server supervision resolver
Skill-advisor plugin bridge environment construction

focus: Determine the context-index.sqlite writer-lock lifetime, explain the 30-minute silent scan with concurrent health responses, and select least-invasive hardening
noveltyJustification: Separated the process-lifetime kernel lock from the scan lease, bounded routine scan phases, showed why concurrent health calls rule out a process-wide deadlock, and identified the existing background job path as the least-invasive hardening.

graphEvents.label: Writer-lock holder and silent scan
Sidecar SQLite kernel lock is held for daemon lifetime
Config-table scan lease is distinct from process lock
Foreground scan emits only a final response
Concurrent health responses rule out process-wide deadlock
Existing background scan job is least-invasive hardening
Database instance lock implementation
Foreground and background scan implementation
Per-client IPC server transport
FTS5 corruption incident and mitigations

focus: Synthesize lease/re-election and lock-arbitration robustness under concurrent multi-session usage
noveltyJustification: Found a concrete stale-owner unlink/heartbeat fencing race, distinguished ownerless adoption from durable re-election, modeled probe/DB-lock amplification, and produced a unified root cause plus ranked hardening sequence.

graphEvents.label: Concurrent daemon lease and lock robustness
Stale owner reclaim lacks pathname fencing
Adoption bridges without durable supervision
Probe storms amplify late DB-lock arbitration
Boundaries reconstruct implicit context and authority
mk-spec-memory launcher lease lifecycle

focus: Adversarially re-verify stale-owner reclamation, heartbeat overwrite, and release-for-discovery semantics
noveltyJustification: Independently confirmed both TOCTOU mechanisms, supplied exact interleavings, and materially narrowed their impact to availability/bookkeeping risk behind the daemon checks, respawn lock, and SQLite writer lock.

Impact is availability churn, not proven data loss

focus: Re-rank the final hardening list, stress-test the canonical runtime context envelope, and assess substantive convergence
noveltyJustification: Materially corrected the final ordering, decomposed the broad envelope into two testable PR-sized handoffs, and identified an unsafe daemon/model socket-authority coupling to avoid.

graphEvents.label: Final hardening order and runtime-envelope feasibility
Lease fencing downgraded below direct low-cost defects
